subject: Project Management
title: The Scrum Master
description: A quick, practical guide to The Scrum Master.

hook.text: The Scrum Master doesn't manage the team. They serve the team.

buildup.text: They remove blockers, facilitate events, and protect the team from outside interference.

discovery.text: The role is 'servant leader' — power through influence, not authority.

twist.text: Many companies hire Scrum Masters, then ask them to be traditional project managers. It fails.

climax.text: The best Scrum Masters make themselves unnecessary — the team eventually self-manages.

punchline.text: Success means the team doesn't need you anymore.

quiz.question: What does 'servant leader' mean for a Scrum Master?
options: Leading through influence and service, not authority
Following orders from the Product Owner
